\section{Introduction}
This paper studies \textbf{widgets} and their \emph{properties}. We cite
prior work~\cite{smith2020} and refer to \ref{sec:method}. Inline math like
$E = mc^2$ stays literal in v1.

\section{Method}
\label{sec:method}
The key identity is
\begin{align}
a + b &= c \\
x     &= y + z
\end{align}
and a single equation:
\[
\int_0^1 x^2 \, dx = \frac{1}{3}
\]

\subsection{Algorithm}
The steps are:
\begin{enumerate}
\item Initialize the state.
\item Iterate until convergence.
\item Return the result.
\end{enumerate}

Properties we rely on:
\begin{itemize}
\item Stability.
\item Monotonicity.
\end{itemize}

\section{Results}
Our results are summarized below.
\begin{tabular}{ll}
Method & Score \\
\hline
Baseline & 0.71 \\
Ours & 0.93 \\
\end{tabular}
